---
title: "Grant & Proposal Development"
---

::: {.topic-badges}
[Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} · **Communication & Dissemination** track · ~1 hour, self-paced
:::

[← Back to the Communication & Dissemination track](communication.qmd){.track-back}

*Draft and tighten the recurring pieces of a proposal — with your science and your verification.*

## What you'll be able to do

- Draft specific aims, summaries, and budget justifications with AI
- Tighten language to a funder's guidelines and page limits
- Verify every claim, citation, and number before it goes in

## Overview

AI can draft a specific-aims page, a summary, or a budget justification from your notes, and reshape text to a funder's format. The science, the feasibility, and the citations are yours to get right.

Funders increasingly expect disclosure of AI assistance and prohibit fabricated content. Treat AI output as a first draft you verify line by line.

## Practice activities

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 1 · Moderate — Draft a specific-aims page</summary>
```

1. Give AI your project's goal, gap, and approach (non-sensitive).
2. Ask for a **one-page specific-aims draft** with a hook, long-term goal, and 2–3 aims.
3. Rewrite the science yourself; verify every cited claim against the source.

```{=html}
</details>
```

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 2 · Moderate — Fit the guidelines</summary>
```

1. Paste a draft section and the funder's instructions.
2. Ask AI to flag where you don't meet the guidance and to tighten to the page limit.
3. Confirm nothing required was dropped.

```{=html}
</details>
```

## Check your readiness

Answer these, then check — your score suggests whether to dive in or skim the readings first.

<div class="readiness-quiz">
<script type="application/json" class="quiz-data">
{"questions": [{"q": "Citations in an AI-drafted aims page should be…", "options": ["Verified against the actual source", "Trusted if formatted correctly", "Left as-is", "Removed"], "answer": 0, "explain": "AI invents plausible citations — open and confirm each one."}, {"q": "Many funders now expect you to…", "options": ["Disclose AI assistance and avoid fabricated content", "Use AI for everything", "Never use AI", "Hide AI use"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Disclosure norms are emerging; fabrication is prohibited."}, {"q": "What stays your responsibility?", "options": ["The science, feasibility, and accuracy", "The font", "The file name", "The submission portal"], "answer": 0, "explain": "You own the substance and the claims."}]}
</script>
</div>

## Recommended readings

From the AI readings library (ask the facilitator for access):

- **AI-Slop, GrantaGate and Bad Writing (Tuhin Chakrabarty)** — the risks of AI-generated proposal text.
- **A Framework for Technical Writing in the Age of LLMs** — writing faster without losing rigor.

## Useful resources

- [NIH How to Apply — Application Guide](https://grants.nih.gov/grants/how-to-apply-application-guide.html) — official NIH proposal guidance.
- [NIH RePORTER](https://reporter.nih.gov) — find funded examples and language.
- [ChatGPT Edu (Drexel AI Tools)](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools) — the approved tool, with data rules.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data rules
Keep unpublished ideas in an **approved** tool; verify all claims and citations before submission. See [Tools & Access](../tools.qmd) and Drexel's [AI Tools page](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools).
:::
